title: alpinia oblongifolia
intro: Alpinia oblongifolia, a species of flowering plant in the ginger family, is native to Southeast Asia and is also known as the 'Naga' or 'Wild Cardamom'. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and aiding in digestive problems. Alpinia oblongifolia is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
